steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb of the conditional clause?

hints.peribitis: 'Peribitis' is the main verb of the result clause, not the 'nisi' (unless) clause
hints.paenitentiam: 'Paenitentiam' is a noun meaning 'repentance' in the accusative

hints.perfect subjunctive: In form these look identical, but in a conditional with future 'peribitis' this is future perfect indicative
hints.pluperfect subjunctive: Pluperfect subjunctive would be 'habuissetis' with the -isse- marker
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'habeatis' — 'habueritis' has the perfect stem

hints.abeo, abire, abii, abitum: These are the principal parts of 'abire' (to go away)
hints.habito, habitare, habitavi, habitatum: These are the principal parts of 'habitare' (to dwell)
hints.teneo, tenere, tenui, tentum: These are the principal parts of 'tenere' (to hold) — similar but distinct

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'paenitentia' — the -am ending marks accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'paenitentia' — the -am ending is accusative singular

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'paenitentiae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'paenitentiae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'paenitentiae' ends in -ae

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'peritis' — 'peribitis' has the future -bi- marker
hints.future perfect indicative: Future perfect would be 'perieritis' — 'peribitis' is simple future

hints.pario, parere, peperi, partum: These are the principal parts of 'parere' (to give birth / produce)
hints.patior, pati, passus sum, —: These are the principal parts of 'pati' (to suffer) — a deponent verb
hints.paro, parare, paravi, paratum: These are the principal parts of 'parare' (to prepare)

hints.accusative: 'Omnes' can be accusative plural, but here it is the subject — nominative
hints.vocative: While addressing 'you all,' 'omnes' functions as a nominative subject here
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'omnibus' — 'omnes' is nominative/accusative

translation: Unless you will have repented, you will all likewise perish
